name: M27 :  Dumbbell Nebula
desc: Messier 27 (M27), commonly known as the Dumbbell Nebula, is a world-famous planetary nebula located roughly 1,200 to 1,360 light-years away in the northern constellation of Vulpecula (The Fox)
camera: FLI (Finger Lakes Instrumentation) ProLine KAF-16803 CCD
location: Observatorio del Teide, Tenerife, Canary Islands

name: NGC 6960 : Veil Nebula
desc: The Veil Nebula is one of the most spectacular and detailed supernova remnants in the night sky. Located approximately 2,100 light-years away in the constellation Cygnus (the Swan), it forms the visible portions of the Cygnus Loop.
camera: FLI (Finger Lakes Instrumentation) ProLine KAF-16803 CCD
location: Observatorio del Teide, Tenerife, Canary Islands
